bp_candidate1

Yimin Zhao

2025-10-13

Load in data and adjust p valuie for multiple testing

There are much more DEGs in Seacell

setwd('/Users/zhaoyimin/Desktop/SCOPE Manuscipt/Figure4 Seacell Palantir/tradeseq new')
palantir_res = readRDS('./bp candidate1/assemble Palantir/res_branchpoint_mono_ery_2lineages.rds')
palantir_res = na.omit(palantir_res)
seacell_res = readRDS('./bp candidate1/assemble Seacell/res_branchpoint_mono_ery_2lineages_allgenes.rds')
seacell_res = na.omit(seacell_res)

palantir_res$padj <- p.adjust(palantir_res$pvalue, method = "BH")
seacell_res$padj <- p.adjust(seacell_res$pvalue, method = "BH")

palantir_degs <- rownames(palantir_res)[palantir_res$padj < 0.05]
seacell_degs <- rownames(seacell_res)[seacell_res$padj < 0.005]

Chi-square test for 2 sets of DEGs

common_degs <- intersect(palantir_degs, seacell_degs)
cat("=== DEG Analysis Results (FDR < 0.05) ===\n")
## === DEG Analysis Results (FDR < 0.05) ===
cat("Number of DEGs in Palantir:", length(palantir_degs), "\n")
## Number of DEGs in Palantir: 38
cat("Number of DEGs in Seacell:", length(seacell_degs), "\n")
## Number of DEGs in Seacell: 230
cat("Number of common DEGs:", length(common_degs), "\n")
## Number of common DEGs: 16
cat("Overlap percentage (relative to Palantir):", 
    round(length(common_degs)/length(palantir_degs)*100, 2), "%\n")
## Overlap percentage (relative to Palantir): 42.11 %
cat("Overlap percentage (relative to Seacell):", 
    round(length(common_degs)/length(seacell_degs)*100, 2), "%\n\n")
## Overlap percentage (relative to Seacell): 6.96 %
# Create 2x2 contingency table for chi-square test
# Get all genes from both datasets
all_genes <- union(rownames(palantir_res), rownames(seacell_res))

# Create logical vectors for DEG status (using adjusted p-values)
palantir_deg <- all_genes %in% palantir_degs
seacell_deg <- all_genes %in% seacell_degs

# Create 2x2 contingency table
contingency_table <- table(palantir_deg, seacell_deg)
colnames(contingency_table) <- c("Seacell_non_DEG", "Seacell_DEG")
rownames(contingency_table) <- c("Palantir_non_DEG", "Palantir_DEG")

# Display the contingency table
cat("=== 2x2 Contingency Table ===\n")
## === 2x2 Contingency Table ===
print(contingency_table)
##                   seacell_deg
## palantir_deg       Seacell_non_DEG Seacell_DEG
##   Palantir_non_DEG           16901         214
##   Palantir_DEG                  22          16
chi_test <- chisq.test(contingency_table)
## Warning in chisq.test(contingency_table): Chi-squared approximation may be
## incorrect
print(chi_test)
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test with Yates' continuity correction
## 
## data:  contingency_table
## X-squared = 448.01, df = 1, p-value < 2.2e-16

Correlation between fold change

library(SingleCellExperiment)
## Loading required package: SummarizedExperiment
## Loading required package: MatrixGenerics
## Loading required package: matrixStats
## 
## Attaching package: 'MatrixGenerics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     colAlls, colAnyNAs, colAnys, colAvgsPerRowSet, colCollapse,
##     colCounts, colCummaxs, colCummins, colCumprods, colCumsums,
##     colDiffs, colIQRDiffs, colIQRs, colLogSumExps, colMadDiffs,
##     colMads, colMaxs, colMeans2, colMedians, colMins, colOrderStats,
##     colProds, colQuantiles, colRanges, colRanks, colSdDiffs, colSds,
##     colSums2, colTabulates, colVarDiffs, colVars, colWeightedMads,
##     colWeightedMeans, colWeightedMedians, colWeightedSds,
##     colWeightedVars, rowAlls, rowAnyNAs, rowAnys, rowAvgsPerColSet,
##     rowCollapse, rowCounts, rowCummaxs, rowCummins, rowCumprods,
##     rowCumsums, rowDiffs, rowIQRDiffs, rowIQRs, rowLogSumExps,
##     rowMadDiffs, rowMads, rowMaxs, rowMeans2, rowMedians, rowMins,
##     rowOrderStats, rowProds, rowQuantiles, rowRanges, rowRanks,
##     rowSdDiffs, rowSds, rowSums2, rowTabulates, rowVarDiffs, rowVars,
##     rowWeightedMads, rowWeightedMeans, rowWeightedMedians,
##     rowWeightedSds, rowWeightedVars
## Loading required package: GenomicRanges
## Loading required package: stats4
## Loading required package: BiocGenerics
## 
## Attaching package: 'BiocGenerics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     anyDuplicated, aperm, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind,
##     colnames, dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find,
##     get, grep, grepl, intersect, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply,
##     match, mget, order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int,
##     Position, rank, rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, setdiff,
##     table, tapply, union, unique, unsplit, which.max, which.min
## Loading required package: S4Vectors
## 
## Attaching package: 'S4Vectors'
## The following object is masked from 'package:utils':
## 
##     findMatches
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     expand.grid, I, unname
## Loading required package: IRanges
## Loading required package: GenomeInfoDb
## Loading required package: Biobase
## Welcome to Bioconductor
## 
##     Vignettes contain introductory material; view with
##     'browseVignettes()'. To cite Bioconductor, see
##     'citation("Biobase")', and for packages 'citation("pkgname")'.
## 
## Attaching package: 'Biobase'
## The following object is masked from 'package:MatrixGenerics':
## 
##     rowMedians
## The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     anyMissing, rowMedians
library(scater)
## Loading required package: scuttle
## Loading required package: ggplot2
setwd('/Users/zhaoyimin/Desktop/SCOPE Manuscipt/Figure4 Seacell Palantir/tradeseq new')
#read in counts
sce_palantir = readRDS("./bp candidate1/assemble Palantir/palantir_allgenes_sce.rds")
sce_seacell = readRDS("./bp candidate1/assemble Seacell/seacell_allgenes_sce.rds")

expr_palantir <- logNormCounts(sce_palantir)[common_degs, ]
expr_seacell <- logNormCounts(sce_seacell)[common_degs, ]

#calculate fold change in palantir
fate_bias_palantir <- metadata(expr_palantir)$fate_bias[,c(1,3)]
cell_class_palantir <- ifelse(fate_bias_palantir[, 1] > fate_bias_palantir[, 2], "Ery", "Mono")
names(cell_class_palantir) <-colnames(sce_palantir)

ery_palantir <- expr_palantir[, names(cell_class_palantir[cell_class_palantir == 'Ery'])]
mono_palantir <- expr_palantir[, names(cell_class_palantir[cell_class_palantir == 'Mono'])]

mean_ery_palantir <- rowMeans(logcounts(ery_palantir))
mean_mono_palantir <- rowMeans(logcounts(mono_palantir))

fold_change_palantir =  mean_ery_palantir / mean_mono_palantir 

#calculate fold change in seacell
fate_bias_seacell <- metadata(expr_seacell)$fate_bias[,c(3,5)]
cell_class_seacell <- ifelse(fate_bias_seacell[, 1] > fate_bias_seacell[, 2], "Ery", "Mono")
names(cell_class_seacell) <-colnames(sce_seacell)

ery_seacell <- expr_seacell[, names(cell_class_seacell[cell_class_seacell == 'Ery'])]
mono_seacell <- expr_seacell[, names(cell_class_seacell[cell_class_seacell == 'Mono'])]

mean_ery_seacell <- rowMeans(logcounts(ery_seacell))
mean_mono_seacell <- rowMeans(logcounts(mono_seacell))

fold_change_seacell =  mean_ery_seacell / mean_mono_seacell
non0_genes =  names(fold_change_seacell[fold_change_seacell != 0])

fold_change_palantir = fold_change_palantir[non0_genes]
fold_change_seacell = fold_change_seacell[non0_genes]

cor(fold_change_palantir, fold_change_seacell)
## [1] 0.8615488
library(ggplot2)

# Combine into a data frame
df <- data.frame(
  palantir = fold_change_palantir,
  seacell = fold_change_seacell
)

# Create scatterplot with linear regression line
ggplot(df, aes(x = palantir, y = seacell)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "darkred", linetype = "solid") +
  labs(
    x = "Fold Change (Palantir)",
    y = "Fold Change (Seacell)",
    title = "Correlation between Palantir and Seacell Fold Changes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Go enrichment analysis

Enrichment analysis for Palantir

library(clusterProfiler)
## 
## clusterProfiler v4.14.6 Learn more at https://yulab-smu.top/contribution-knowledge-mining/
## 
## Please cite:
## 
## S Xu, E Hu, Y Cai, Z Xie, X Luo, L Zhan, W Tang, Q Wang, B Liu, R Wang,
## W Xie, T Wu, L Xie, G Yu. Using clusterProfiler to characterize
## multiomics data. Nature Protocols. 2024, 19(11):3292-3320
## 
## Attaching package: 'clusterProfiler'
## The following object is masked from 'package:IRanges':
## 
##     slice
## The following object is masked from 'package:S4Vectors':
## 
##     rename
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
## Loading required package: AnnotationDbi
## 
## Attaching package: 'AnnotationDbi'
## The following object is masked from 'package:clusterProfiler':
## 
##     select
## 
genes_palantir <- all_genes[palantir_deg]

# Convert to Entrez IDs
gene_ids <- bitr(genes_palantir, fromType="SYMBOL", toType="ENTREZID", 
                 OrgDb="org.Hs.eg.db")
## 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns
## Warning in bitr(genes_palantir, fromType = "SYMBOL", toType = "ENTREZID", :
## 10.53% of input gene IDs are fail to map...
# GO enrichment
ego <- enrichGO(gene = gene_ids$ENTREZID,
                OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
                ont = "ALL",
                pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                pvalueCutoff = 0.01,
                readable = TRUE)

dotplot(ego, showCategory=10)

Enrichment analysis for Seacell

library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
genes_seacell <- all_genes[seacell_deg]

# Convert to Entrez IDs
gene_ids_seacell <- bitr(genes_seacell, fromType="SYMBOL", toType="ENTREZID", 
                 OrgDb="org.Hs.eg.db")
## 'select()' returned 1:many mapping between keys and columns
## Warning in bitr(genes_seacell, fromType = "SYMBOL", toType = "ENTREZID", : 1.3%
## of input gene IDs are fail to map...
# GO enrichment
ego_seacell <- enrichGO(gene = gene_ids_seacell$ENTREZID,
                OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
                ont = "ALL",
                pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                readable = TRUE)

dotplot(ego_seacell, showCategory=10)

Correlation of p value for overlapping and significant pathways of 2 datasets

There are only 3 overlapping pathways.

library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following object is masked from 'package:AnnotationDbi':
## 
##     select
## The following object is masked from 'package:Biobase':
## 
##     combine
## The following objects are masked from 'package:GenomicRanges':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, union
## The following object is masked from 'package:GenomeInfoDb':
## 
##     intersect
## The following objects are masked from 'package:IRanges':
## 
##     collapse, desc, intersect, setdiff, slice, union
## The following objects are masked from 'package:S4Vectors':
## 
##     first, intersect, rename, setdiff, setequal, union
## The following objects are masked from 'package:BiocGenerics':
## 
##     combine, intersect, setdiff, union
## The following object is masked from 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     count
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

df_palantir <- as.data.frame(ego) %>%
  select(ID, Description, pvalue, p.adjust)

df_seacell <- as.data.frame(ego_seacell) %>%
  select(ID, Description, pvalue, p.adjust)

# --- Define "significant" using each object's own cutoff ---
cut_palantir <- ego@pvalueCutoff
cut_seacell  <- ego_seacell@pvalueCutoff

sig_palantir <- df_palantir %>% filter(p.adjust <= cut_palantir)
sig_seacell  <- df_seacell  %>% filter(p.adjust <= cut_seacell)

# --- Overlap by GO ID (robust across ontologies) ---
overlap <- inner_join(sig_palantir, sig_seacell, by = "ID",
                      suffix = c("_palantir", "_seacell"))

# If you prefer to keep one Description column:
overlap <- overlap %>%
  mutate(Description = coalesce(Description_palantir, Description_seacell)) %>%
  select(ID, Description, pvalue_palantir, p.adjust_palantir,
         pvalue_seacell,  p.adjust_seacell)

# --- Correlations ---
# Option A (recommended): use -log10 adjusted p-values
overlap2 <- overlap %>%
  mutate(mlog10_padj_palantir = -log10(p.adjust_palantir),
         mlog10_padj_seacell  = -log10(p.adjust_seacell)) %>%
  filter(is.finite(mlog10_padj_palantir), is.finite(mlog10_padj_seacell))

corr_spear <- cor.test(overlap2$mlog10_padj_palantir,
                       overlap2$mlog10_padj_seacell,
                       method = "spearman")
## Warning in cor.test.default(overlap2$mlog10_padj_palantir,
## overlap2$mlog10_padj_seacell, : Cannot compute exact p-value with ties
corr_pear  <- cor.test(overlap2$mlog10_padj_palantir,
                       overlap2$mlog10_padj_seacell,
                       method = "pearson")

cat(sprintf(
  "Overlap: %d GO terms\nSpearman rho = %.3f (p=%.3g)\nPearson r = %.3f (p=%.3g)\n",
  nrow(overlap2), corr_spear$estimate, corr_spear$p.value,
  corr_pear$estimate,  corr_pear$p.value))
## Overlap: 3 GO terms
## Spearman rho = -0.500 (p=0.667)
## Pearson r = -0.500 (p=0.667)
# Option B (if you truly want raw p-values): just swap to pvalue_* columns
# and re-run the two cor.tests above.

# --- Scatter plot of concordance (adjusted p-values) ---
ggplot(overlap2, aes(x = mlog10_padj_palantir, y = mlog10_padj_seacell)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = Description),
                  max.overlaps = 15, size = 3) +
  labs(
    x = "-log10(adj p) Palantir",
    y = "-log10(adj p) Seacell",
    title = "Concordance of GO Enrichment (overlapping significant terms)",
    subtitle = sprintf("Overlap: %d terms | Spearman rho = %.3f (p=%.3g)",
                       nrow(overlap2), corr_spear$estimate, corr_spear$p.value)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Correlation of p value for all pathways of 2 datasets

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

ego <- enrichGO(gene = gene_ids$ENTREZID,
                OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
                ont = "ALL",
                pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                pvalueCutoff = 1,
                readable = TRUE)

ego_seacell <- enrichGO(gene = gene_ids_seacell$ENTREZID,
                OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
                ont = "ALL",
                pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                pvalueCutoff = 1,
                readable = TRUE)


ego_palantir_all_df <- as.data.frame(ego)
ego_seacell_all_df <- as.data.frame(ego_seacell)

intersection_pathways <- intersect(rownames(ego_palantir_all_df), rownames(ego_seacell_all_df))
ego_palantir_all_df <- ego_palantir_all_df[intersection_pathways,]
ego_seacell_all_df <- ego_seacell_all_df[intersection_pathways,]

vec1 <- -log10(ego_palantir_all_df$pvalue)*sign(ego_palantir_all_df$FoldEnrichment)
vec2 <- -log10(ego_seacell_all_df$pvalue)*sign(ego_seacell_all_df$FoldEnrichment)
df_pathway_pval = data.frame('palantir' = vec1, 'seacell' = vec2)

stats::cor(vec1, vec2)
## [1] 0.1142806
p = ggplot(df_pathway_pval, aes(x = palantir, y = seacell)) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 20) + 
  geom_point()+
  theme(panel.grid.major=element_blank(),panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),panel.background=element_blank())+
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm') +  # optional: a visible line color
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = "r = 0.114", hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 6) +
  labs(title = "Pathways in branchpoints",
       x = "-log10 pval Palantir",
       y = "-log10 pval Seacell")+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) 

p
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

#ggsave('/Users/zhaoyimin/Desktop/SCOPE Manuscipt/Figure4 Seacell Palantir/tradeseq new/bp candidate1/assemble Palantir/pathway.png',p, dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6)
